text: Standing here, I sense both anticipation and dislocation. The first space feels quiet, almost expectant, imbued with the sterile safety of institutional design—the presence of a fire hose and modern lighting suggesting order but also an uncanny emptiness. Moving into the second scene, the bright colors and bold graphics beckon with the promise of discovery and innovation. Surrounded by words and artifacts, I feel swept up into a dialogue between past and future, human and machine—a liminal state between curiosity and reflective uncertainty. Each step compels me to ask: how do we truly anchor ourselves in spaces poised on the edge of transformation?
